title: The Power of a Name
carnegie_principle: Remember that a person's name is to that person the sweetest and most important sound in any language.
modern_context: Personalizing digital openers (Slack, Email, LinkedIn) to cut through the noise.
practice_prompt: You are messaging a busy stakeholder, Sarah, for the first time. Open the conversation naturally using her name and a clear reason for reaching out.

title: Smile on Camera
carnegie_principle: Smile.
modern_context: Projecting warmth and presence in high-stakes video calls even when you're tired.
practice_prompt: Join this mock Zoom meeting and introduce yourself. Focus on maintaining a genuine smile and steady eye contact with the camera lens.
success_criteria: Eyes fixed on camera, not the screen

title: Kill the Filler
carnegie_principle: Be a good listener. Encourage others to talk about themselves.
modern_context: Using strategic pauses to eliminate 'um', 'ah', and 'like' for more authority.
practice_prompt: Explain your current project in 60 seconds. Every time you want to say a filler word, take a breath and pause instead.

title: Become Genuinely Interested
carnegie_principle: Become genuinely interested in other people.
modern_context: Active listening and room-reading in a hybrid work environment.
practice_prompt: Ask three insightful follow-up questions to the AI avatar after they describe their weekend trip to Japan.

title: Let Others Talk
carnegie_principle: Let the other person do a great deal of the talking.
modern_context: Facilitating vs dominating meetings. Learning when to yield the floor.
practice_prompt: You are leading a stand-up. Practice a transition that invites a quieter teammate to share their progress without putting them on the spot.

title: Avoid Criticism
carnegie_principle: Don't criticize, condemn or complain.
modern_context: Delivering radical candor via the SBI (Situation, Behavior, Impact) framework without triggering defensiveness.
practice_prompt: A colleague missed a deadline. Give them feedback using the SBI framework without being critical of their character.

title: Talk in Terms of Their Interests
carnegie_principle: Talk in terms of the other person's interests.
modern_context: Stakeholder management. Framing your request as a win for their specific KPIs.
practice_prompt: Pitch a new software tool to a CFO who only cares about cost reduction and ROI. Frame your entire pitch around those two points.

title: The Sincere Compliment
carnegie_principle: Give honest and sincere appreciation.
modern_context: Specific vs generic praise. How to compliment a peer so it actually lands.
practice_prompt: Observe the AI avatar's 'presentation' and give one specific, sincere compliment about a particular detail of their performance.

title: Admit Mistakes Quickly
carnegie_principle: If you are wrong, admit it quickly and emphatically.
modern_context: Building psychological safety. Leading with vulnerability when a project slips.
practice_prompt: You made a mistake in the quarterly report. Practice a 30-second Slack video message to the team admitting the error and outlining the fix.

title: The 2-Minute Pitch
carnegie_principle: Dramatize your ideas.
modern_context: Hook → Insight → Ask. The foundational structure of persuasive leadership.
practice_prompt: You have two minutes with a high-level executive. Pitch your vision for the company's next AI initiative.

title: Slack Tone: The Digital Handshake
carnegie_principle: Make the other person feel important - and do it sincerely.
modern_context: Mastering the nuance of async text communication. Avoiding 'Hey' without context.
practice_prompt: Rewrite a blunt request for data into a warm, context-rich message that makes the recipient feel valued.

title: LinkedIn Voice: Professional Sincerity
carnegie_principle: Arouse in the other person an eager want.
modern_context: Writing a cold reach-out that isn't spam. Building genuine bridges.
practice_prompt: Draft a personalized connection request to a mentor you admire. Explain why you want to connect in terms of their work.

title: Async Video: Authority + Warmth
carnegie_principle: Be hearty in your approbation and lavish in your praise.
modern_context: Using Loom or Slack video for feedback. Using your face and voice to build trust async.
practice_prompt: Record a 60-second video update on a successful project milestone. Focus on praising the team's specific contributions.

title: Email That Lands
carnegie_principle: Call attention to people's mistakes indirectly.
modern_context: Writing tough emails that don't burn bridges. Managing up via text.
practice_prompt: Email a manager who forgot to include you in a critical meeting. Point it out without being accusatory.

title: The Power of One-on-Ones
carnegie_principle: Show respect for the other person's opinions. Never say, 'You're wrong.'
modern_context: Structuring high-trust recurring meetings. Listening for what's NOT being said.
practice_prompt: Start a 1:1 with a direct report who has been underperforming lately. Create a safe space for them to speak first.

title: The Art of Disagreement
carnegie_principle: The only way to get the best of an argument is to avoid it.
modern_context: Navigating technical or strategic disagreements in public channels.
practice_prompt: Disagree with a colleague's technical proposal in a mock Slack thread. Validate their points before offering your alternative.

title: Leading Through Uncertainty
carnegie_principle: Give the other person a fine reputation to live up to.
modern_context: Communicating during a pivot or reorg. Keeping the team focused on the mission.
practice_prompt: Give a short 'all-hands' update about a difficult project pivot. Reaffirm the team's strengths.

title: The Sincere No
carnegie_principle: Make the other person happy about doing the thing you suggest.
modern_context: Setting boundaries without losing influence. Saying 'no' to a project but 'yes' to the person.
practice_prompt: Turn down a request to join a new committee while maintaining the relationship with the person who asked.

title: Handling Digital Confrontation
carnegie_principle: Begin in a friendly way.
modern_context: De-escalating a heated thread. When to take it off-line.
practice_prompt: A client is angry in a shared Slack channel. Write a response that de-escalates and moves the conversation to a 1:1 call.

title: Mastering the Hybrid Meeting
carnegie_principle: Ask questions instead of giving direct orders.
modern_context: Ensuring remote participants feel as included as those in the room.
practice_prompt: You are facilitating a hybrid brainstorm. Explicitly pull in a remote participant who hasn't spoken yet.

title: Authentic Executive Presence
carnegie_principle: Let the other person feel that the idea is his or hers.
modern_context: Leading without appearing to command. The 'Inception' method of leadership.
practice_prompt: Guide an AI executive to your proposed strategy using only Socratic questioning.

title: High-Stakes Negotiation
carnegie_principle: Try honestly to see things from the other person's point of view.
modern_context: Salary or contract negotiation. Finding the 'Third Way' where everyone wins.
practice_prompt: Negotiate a 15% budget increase with a skeptical AI department head.

title: The Sincerity of Leadership
carnegie_principle: Use encouragement. Make the fault seem easy to correct.
modern_context: Coaching a high-performer through a slump. Moving from 'Manager' to 'Coach'.
practice_prompt: Deliver a coaching session to a star player who just failed a major launch.

title: Complex Conflict Resolution
carnegie_principle: Be sympathetic with the other person's ideas and desires.
modern_context: Mediating a deep rift between two powerful stakeholders.
practice_prompt: Practice the first 5 minutes of a mediation session between two AI co-founders who aren't speaking.

title: Visionary Storytelling
carnegie_principle: Appeal to the nobler motives.
modern_context: Rallying a global team around a difficult, long-term mission.
practice_prompt: Deliver a 'Founder's Vision' talk for a project that won't show results for 3 years.

title: The Radical Candor Reset
carnegie_principle: Talk about your own mistakes before criticizing the other person.
modern_context: Correcting toxic culture by leading with your own growth areas.
practice_prompt: Address a team-wide burnout issue by first sharing your own struggles with work-life balance.

title: Negotiating with Power
carnegie_principle: Let the other person save face.
modern_context: How to win a strategic argument with your CEO without embarrassing them.
practice_prompt: Propose a complete reversal of a CEO's pet project in a private meeting.

title: Public Authenticity
carnegie_principle: Dramatize your ideas.
modern_context: Handling Q&A in front of 500+ people. Projecting calm under fire.
practice_prompt: Answer three difficult, confrontational audience questions after a keynote.

title: Building Lasting Influence
carnegie_principle: Praise the slightest improvement and praise every improvement.
modern_context: Cultivating long-term champions in your organization.
practice_prompt: Practice a series of small, authentic 'nudges' that build up a junior colleague over time.

title: The Authentic Leader's Legacy
carnegie_principle: Arouse in the other person an eager want.
modern_context: The final capstone. Defining your own leadership philosophy.
practice_prompt: Summarize your core leadership values and how you will apply them to your next decade of work.
